input.query: What are the legal requirements for data breach notification in California?
What are the legal requirements for data breach notification in California?

documents.title: CCPA Section 1798.82
documents.content: A business shall notify any California resident whose unencrypted personal information was, or is reasonably believed to have been, acquired by an unauthorized person.

input.query: What are the legal requirements for data breach notification in California?

documents.title: CPRA Amendments 2023
documents.content: CPRA expands notification requirements to include delayed notifications, specific content requirements, and attorney general reporting for breaches affecting 500+ residents.

input.query: What are the legal requirements for data breach notification in California?

documents.title: Cal. Civ. Code 1798.82(d)
documents.content: Notification shall be made in the most expedient time possible and without unreasonable delay, consistent with legitimate needs of law enforcement.

input.query: What are the legal requirements for data breach notification in California?

documents.title: CA AG Breach Notification Guidelines
documents.content: Businesses must submit a sample copy of the breach notice to the Attorney General when the breach affects 500+ California residents.

input.query: What are the legal requirements for data breach notification in California?

documents.title: HIPAA 45 CFR 164.404
documents.content: Covered entities must notify affected individuals within 60 days of discovery. Interacts with state law when both apply — stricter standard prevails.

input.query: What are the legal requirements for data breach notification in California?

documents.title: Johnson v. Adobe (2023)
documents.content: Court held that statutory damages are available even without actual harm. Standing requires demonstrable injury-in-fact under TransUnion v. Ramirez.

messages.content: What are the legal requirements for data breach notification in California?

output.text: Under California law (CCPA/CPRA), businesses must notify affected California residents whose unencrypted personal information was acquired by an unauthorized person. Notification must be made 'in the most expedient time possible and without unreasonable delay.' For breaches affecting 500+ residents, a sample notice must be submitted to the Attorney General.

messages.content: Refine and format the answer with specific requirements.

output.text:
California Data Breach Notification Requirements:

1. Timing: Most expedient time possible, without unreasonable delay, consistent with law enforcement needs.
2. Content: (a) Business name and contact info, (b) Types of personal information involved, (c) Date of breach (or estimated date range), (d) Whether notification was delayed due to law enforcement, (e) Description of measures taken.
3. AG Reporting: Submit sample notice to CA Attorney General if 500+ residents affected.
4. Method: Written notice to last known address, or electronic notice with consent.
5. Federal Interplay: HIPAA (60-day federal standard) and state law both apply — stricter standard prevails.
6. Penalties: Statutory damages available even without actual harm (Johnson v. Adobe).
